llcFilesWith: Arizona Secretary of State (trade name registration; a separate agency from the Corporation Commission that holds the LLC)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Arizona Secretary of State (the same trade name registration)
feeBasis: The $10 is the Secretary of State's fee for a trade name registration, good for five years. The split matters: the LLC itself is formed at the Corporation Commission, but the trade name is a separate Secretary of State filing.
protectionNote: A trade name registration is public notice that you use the name; the Secretary of State's own guidance says it grants no exclusive right and no trademark protection. It has one practical effect: a registered trade name is part of the A.R.S. § 29-3112 distinguishability test, so while it is active the Corporation Commission will not register a new entity name that fails to be distinguishable from it.
searchNote: Arizona trade names live at the Secretary of State, not the Corporation Commission, so they are searched in a different database from the entity business search. Clearing a name fully in Arizona means checking both.
notes: Arizona splits the work across two agencies: the LLC is formed at the Corporation Commission, while a trade name is registered at the Secretary of State. A trade name is optional, cheap, and lasts five years, but registering one does not stop others from using a similar name. Its only teeth come from being part of the entity distinguishability test at the Commission.

sources.title: Registration of trade names with the Secretary of State
